[1656]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 寄生字王孫 - Prince Wensun, who is dependent or favored.
  • 長益秀美 - As he grew older, he became increasingly graceful and beautiful.
  • 十四入郡庠 - Entered the prefectural school at the age of fourteen.
  • 自擇偶 - Chose his own spouse.
  • 父桂庵有妹二娘 - Father Gui'an has a younger sister named Erniang.
  • 適鄭秀才子僑 - Married Mr. Zheng, a scholar.
  • 生女閨秀 - Gave birth to a refined daughter.
  • 心切愛慕 - Deeply admired or deeply loved.
  • 積久寢食俱廢 - Over time, both sleep and food were neglected.
  • 苦研詰之 - Questioned him rigorously.
  • 遂以實告 - Then told the truth.
  • 遣冰於鄭 - Sent a matchmaker to Zheng.
  • 性方謹 - Character is upright and cautious.
  • 中表為嫌卻之 - Rejected due to suspicion of being cousins.
  • 愈病 - Worsened his illness.
  • 計無所出 - Has no plan.
  • 陰婉致二娘 - Secretly and gently brought Erniang.
  • 但求閨秀一臨存之 - Only request for the refined lady to visit and comfort him.
  • 聞益怒 - Became even more angry upon hearing.
  • 出惡聲焉 - Uttered harsh words there.
  • 聽之而已 - Listened to it and nothing more.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 寄生 - In this context, suggests a dependent or favored position, possibly indicating a noble or privileged background.
  • 郡中名士 - Refers to a respected scholar or intellectual in the prefecture, highlighting the social status and reputation of the family.
  • 襁褓認父 - Indicates a special bond or recognition that was established very early in life, possibly through a significant event or circumstance.
  • 夙惠 - Emphasizes the early recognition of the individual's exceptional qualities or potential.
  • 郡庠 - The prefectural school was an important educational institution during the Ming and Qing dynasties, indicating that the individual received formal education.
  • 自擇偶 - Suggests a level of autonomy in choosing a spouse, which was not common in traditional Chinese society.
  • 中表 - Refers to cousins, and the rejection based on this relationship reflects the importance of kinship and social ties in traditional Chinese society.
  • 陰婉 - Indicates a subtle and gentle approach, often used in diplomatic or sensitive situations.

[1657]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 大姓 - Refers to a prominent or influential family in a region.
  • 五女皆美 - Means 'five daughters, all beautiful,' emphasizing the family's aesthetic appeal.
  • 尤冠诸姊 - Means 'especially surpassing her sisters,' highlighting the exceptional beauty of the youngest daughter.
  • 择婿未字 - Means 'choosing a husband but not yet married,' indicating that the daughter is still unmarried.
  • 上墓 - Means 'going to the ancestral tomb,' a common activity for paying respects to ancestors.
  • 舆中窥见 - Means 'saw from within the carriage,' suggesting a glimpse from a distance.
  • 白母 - Means 'reported to her mother,' indicating the young man's action after seeing the daughter.
  • 沈知其意 - Means 'understood his intention,' implying the mother's awareness of the young man's interest.
  • 诣王所 - Means 'went to the young man's place,' indicating the matchmaker's visit.
  • 病 - Here, it likely means 'illness' or 'unwell,' suggesting the young man's current state.
  • 医不对症 - Means 'the medicine does not match the illness,' a metaphor for the mismatch between the matchmaker's proposal and the young man's condition.
  • 医良否 - Means 'whether the doctor is good,' a question about the quality of the matchmaker's proposal.
  • 召和而缓至 - Means 'call for a good matchmaker and wait patiently,' suggesting a more appropriate approach.
  • 执其人以求之 - Means 'hold onto the person and seek them out,' implying persistence.
  • 守死而待之 - Means 'wait until death to find them,' a hyperbolic expression of extreme persistence.
  • 不亦痴乎 - Means 'isn't that foolish?' a rhetorical question expressing disbelief.
  • 但天下之医无愈和者 - Means 'but there is no doctor in the world better than this one,' emphasizing the uniqueness of the matchmaker.
  • 何见之不广也 - Means 'how narrow your vision is,' a criticism of the young man's limited perspective.
  • 反身向壁 - Means 'turned his back to the wall,' indicating the young man's disinterest or refusal.
  • 不复听矣 - Means 'no longer listened,' showing the young man's final decision.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 大姓 - Refers to a prominent family in a region, often with significant social and economic influence.
  • 上墓 - A traditional Chinese practice of visiting ancestral tombs to pay respects and offer sacrifices.
  • 媒媪 - Refers to a matchmaker, typically an older woman who facilitates marriages.
  • 王孙 - Refers to a young nobleman or prince, often used to denote someone of high social status.
  • 芸娘 - Likely a name, possibly referring to a character in the story or a family member.

Proper Nouns

  • 张氏 - The name of the prominent family.
  • 五可 - The name of the youngest daughter.
  • 王孙 - The name or title of the young nobleman.
  • 芸娘 - The name of the young man's mother.
  • 于氏 - The name of the matchmaker.

[1658]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 一日 - Literally 'one day,' often used to introduce a specific incident or event.
  • 沉痼中 - A compound term meaning 'in a deep illness' or 'suffering from a chronic ailment.'
  • 所思之人至矣 - A literary expression meaning 'the person I have been thinking about has arrived.'
  • 喜極，躍然而起 - A vivid description of extreme joy leading to sudden action.
  • 遂與要誓 - Means 'then they made an oath' or 'then they exchanged vows.'
  • 遽然而覺 - Means 'suddenly woke up' or 'awoke abruptly.'
  • 回思聲容笑貌，宛在目中 - A poetic expression meaning 'when I recall the sound, appearance, and smile, they seem to be right before my eyes.'
  • 陰念 - Means 'secretly thought' or 'pondered in private.'
  • 何必求所難遘 - A rhetorical question meaning 'why seek what is hard to come by?'
  • 急欲媒之 - Means 'hurriedly wanted to arrange a match' or 'eagerly sought to arrange a marriage.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 王孫 - Refers to a young nobleman or a member of the royal family.
  • 五可 - The name of a woman in the story, likely a fictional character.
  • 神仙不啻也 - A metaphor comparing the woman to a celestial being, emphasizing her beauty and grace.
  • 要誓 - Refers to the act of making a solemn promise or oath, often associated with romantic or marital commitments.
  • 媒之 - Refers to the practice of arranging marriages through a matchmaker, a common tradition in ancient China.

Proper Nouns

  • 王孫 - A nobleman or royal family member.
  • 五可 - The name of the woman in the story.
  • 閨秀 - Refers to women of the upper class, often associated with refinement and virtue.

[1659]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 王孫恐 - Wang Sun, a noble or aristocratic figure, with '恐' indicating fear or anxiety.
  • 不的 - Means 'uncertain' or 'inaccurate,' suggesting that Wang Sun had doubts about his dream.
  • 托鄰妪 - '托' means 'to entrust,' and '鄰妪' refers to a neighborly old woman, indicating that Wang Sun sought help from a trusted elder.
  • 素识张氏者 - '素识' means 'long acquainted with,' and '张氏' refers to the Zhang family, indicating that the old woman had prior knowledge of the Zhang family.
  • 伪以他故诣之 - '伪' means 'pretending,' '以他故' means 'under the pretext of some other reason,' and '诣之' means 'to visit him,' suggesting that the old woman pretended to have another reason for visiting Zhang.
  • 嘱其潜相五可 - '嘱' means 'to instruct,' '潜相' means 'to secretly observe,' and '五可' refers to a woman named Wukě, indicating that Wang Sun asked the old woman to secretly observe Wukě.
  • 五可方病 - '方病' means 'was just ill,' indicating that Wukě was sick at the time.
  • 靠枕支颐 - '靠枕' means 'leaning on a pillow,' and '支颐' means 'resting one's chin on one's hand,' describing a posture.
  • 婀娜之态 - '婀娜' means 'graceful' or 'delicate,' describing Wukě's appearance.
  • 倾绝一世 - Means 'unparalleled in the world,' emphasizing Wukě's exceptional beauty.
  • 女默然弄带 - '默然' means 'silently,' and '弄带' means 'playing with her belt,' describing Wukě's reaction.
  • 不作一语 - Means 'did not say a word,' indicating that Wukě remained silent.
  • 母代答曰 - '母' refers to Wukě's mother, and '代答' means 'answered on her behalf,' indicating that Wukě's mother spoke for her.
  • 非病也 - Means 'it's not illness,' indicating that Wukě was not actually sick.
  • 连日与爹娘负气耳 - '连日' means 'for several days,' '与爹娘负气' means 'arguing with her parents,' indicating that Wukě had been upset with her parents.
  • 必如王家寄生者方嫁 - '必如' means 'must be like,' '王家寄生者' refers to a woman from the Wang family named Jìshēng, and '方嫁' means 'only then will she marry,' indicating that Wukě wanted to marry someone like Jìshēng.
  • 是为母者劝之急 - '是为母者' means 'her mother,' and '劝之急' means 'urged her urgently,' indicating that Wukě's mother was pressuring her to marry.
  • 遂作意不食数日矣 - '遂' means 'then,' '作意' means 'intended,' '不食' means 'not eating,' and '数日矣' means 'for several days already,' indicating that Wukě had stopped eating for several days.
  • 妪笑曰 - '妪' refers to the old woman, and '笑曰' means 'laughed and said,' indicating that the old woman laughed and spoke.
  • 娘子若配王郎 - '娘子' means 'young lady,' '若' means 'if,' '配' means 'to marry,' and '王郎' refers to Wang Sun, indicating that the old woman said if Wukě married Wang Sun.
  • 真是玉人成双也 - '真是' means 'indeed,' '玉人' means 'beautiful person,' and '成双' means 'become a pair,' indicating that the old woman said it would be a perfect match.
  • 渠若见五娘 - '渠' means 'he,' and '见五娘' means 'see Wukě,' indicating that Wang Sun would see Wukě.
  • 恐又憔悴死矣 - '恐' means 'fear,' '又' means 'again,' '憔悴' means 'wasted,' and '死矣' means 'will die,' indicating that Wang Sun might die of worry again.
  • 我归即令倩冰 - '我归' means 'I will return,' '即令' means 'will immediately order,' and '倩冰' means 'arrange a matchmaker,' indicating that the old woman would arrange a matchmaker.
  • 如何 - Means 'how about it,' indicating that the old woman asked for Wang Sun's opinion.
  • 五可止之曰 - '五可' refers to Wukě, '止之' means 'stopped her,' and '曰' means 'said,' indicating that Wukě stopped the old woman and spoke.
  • 姥勿尔 - '姥' means 'aunt,' '勿尔' means 'don't do that,' indicating that Wukě told the old woman not to do it.
  • 恐其不谐 - '恐' means 'fear,' '其' means 'it,' and '不谐' means 'will not be successful,' indicating that Wukě was afraid the match would not succeed.
  • 益增笑耳 - '益' means 'more,' '增' means 'add,' and '笑耳' means 'laugh,' indicating that it would only add to the ridicule.
  • 妪锐然以必成自任 - '锐然' means 'determinedly,' '以必成' means 'to ensure success,' and '自任' means 'take responsibility,' indicating that the old woman took it upon herself to ensure success.
  • 五可方微笑 - '方' means 'just,' '微笑' means 'smiled slightly,' indicating that Wukě smiled slightly.
  • 妪归复命 - '归' means 'returned,' '复命' means 'reported back,' indicating that the old woman returned to report.
  • 一如媒媪言 - '一如' means 'just as,' '媒媪' means 'matchmaker woman,' and '言' means 'said,' indicating that the old woman reported exactly as the matchmaker had said.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 王孙 - Refers to a noble or aristocratic figure, indicating the social status of Wang Sun.
  • 张氏 - Refers to the Zhang family, indicating the family to which Wukě belongs.
  • 王家寄生 - Refers to a woman from the Wang family named Jìshēng, indicating the type of woman Wukě desires to marry.
  • 媒媪 - Refers to a matchmaker woman, indicating the traditional role of matchmakers in arranging marriages.

Proper Nouns

  • 王孙 - Refers to Wang Sun, a noble or aristocratic figure.
  • 张氏 - Refers to the Zhang family.
  • 王家寄生 - Refers to a woman from the Wang family named Jìshēng.

[1661]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 失意 - Means 'disappointed' or 'dejected.'
  • 悔闷欲死 - A colloquial expression meaning 'regretful and sad to the point of death.'
  • 雞骨支床 - A metaphor for being extremely weak or frail.
  • 唐突 - Means 'rude' or 'forward.'
  • 先炊者先餐 - A proverb meaning 'the first to cook is the first to eat,' suggesting that those who take initiative will benefit first.
  • 守頭巾戒 - A metaphor for adhering strictly to conventional norms or rules.
  • 殺吾娇女 - A hyperbolic expression meaning 'ruin my precious daughter.'
  • 夫妻反目 - Means 'husband and wife quarrel' or 'marital discord.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 王孫 - Refers to a noble or aristocratic young man.
  • 桂庵 - A name that may refer to a specific person or place, but without additional context, it is unclear.
  • 張氏 - Refers to a family or individual named Zhang.
  • 京都皇子 - Refers to a prince of the imperial capital.
  • 鄭子侨 - A name that may refer to a specific person, but without additional context, it is unclear.
  • 二娘 - A term of respect for a woman, possibly a wife or elder sister.
  • 鄭恚曰 - Indicates that Zheng (the husband) is angry and speaks.
  • 若所生女 - Refers to 'the daughter you have given birth to.'

Proper Nouns

  • 王孫 - A noble or aristocratic young man.
  • 桂庵 - A name that may refer to a specific person or place.
  • 張氏 - A family or individual named Zhang.
  • 京都皇子 - A prince of the imperial capital.
  • 鄭子侨 - A name that may refer to a specific person.
  • 二娘 - A term of respect for a woman, possibly a wife or elder sister.
  • 鄭 - The husband, Zheng.
  • 若所生女 - The daughter of the speaker.

[1662]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 故与女言 - Indicates a past action, '故' means 'past,' '与女言' means 'to speak with the girl,' suggesting a deliberate or purposeful conversation.
  • 将使仍归王孙若为媵 - Means 'will make her return to the prince if she were to be a concubine' or 'will make her return to the prince as a concubine.'
  • 俯首不言 - '俯首' means 'lowering one's head,' '不言' means 'not speaking,' together it conveys a sense of submission or reluctance.
  • 病渐瘥 - '病' means 'illness,' '渐' means 'gradually,' '瘥' means 'to improve,' so '病渐瘥' means 'her illness gradually improved.'
  • 亲迎有日矣 - '亲迎' means 'the ceremony of the bridegroom personally going to the bride's home to fetch her,' '有日矣' means 'has a date now,' indicating that the wedding is scheduled.
  • 伪欲归宁 - '伪' means 'pretending,' '欲' means 'wanting,' '归宁' means 'returning to one's parents' home,' so '伪欲归宁' means 'pretending to want to return to her parents' home.'
  • 以毡贴地而入 - '以毡贴地' means 'with a mat placed on the ground,' '而入' means 'and enter,' so '以毡贴地而入' means 'to enter with a mat placed on the ground.'
  • 鼓乐已集 - '鼓乐' means 'drums and music,' '已集' means 'already gathered,' so '鼓乐已集' means 'the drums and music are already gathered.'
  • 从仆叱令吹擂 - '从仆' means 'servants,' '叱令' means 'ordered,' '吹擂' means 'to play the drums and gongs,' so '从仆叱令吹擂' means 'the servants ordered to play the drums and gongs.'
  • 人声沸聒 - '人声' means 'voices,' '沸聒' means 'loud and noisy,' so '人声沸聒' means 'the voices were loud and noisy.'
  • 骇极欲奔 - '骇极' means 'extremely frightened,' '欲奔' means 'wanting to run away,' so '骇极欲奔' means 'extremely frightened and wanting to run away.'
  • 交拜 - Means 'to bow to each other,' a ritual act in traditional Chinese weddings.
  • 径坐青庐 - '径坐' means 'to sit directly,' '青庐' means 'a green pavilion,' a term for the bridal chamber.
  • 闺秀 - Refers to a well-bred and educated woman.
  • 举家皇乱 - '举家' means 'the whole family,' '皇乱' means 'in a state of confusion,' so '举家皇乱' means 'the whole family was in a state of confusion.'
  • 相对筹思 - '相对' means 'facing each other,' '筹思' means 'to deliberate,' so '相对筹思' means 'to deliberate facing each other.'
  • 喜怒俱无所施 - '喜怒' means 'joy and anger,' '俱' means 'both,' '无所施' means 'have no place to express,' so '喜怒俱无所施' means 'have no way to express joy or anger.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 王孙 - Refers to a noble youth or prince.
  • 媵 - In ancient China, a媵 was a concubine or secondary wife.
  • 亲迎 - A traditional Chinese wedding custom where the groom personally goes to the bride's home to fetch her.
  • 归宁 - A term for a married woman returning to her parents' home, often for visits or during festivals.
  • 青庐 - A term for the bridal chamber, often decorated with green cloth.
  • 闺秀 - Refers to a well-bred and educated woman, often used to describe a virtuous and accomplished woman.

Proper Nouns

  • 二娘 - A name, likely referring to a woman of a certain status or role.
  • 郑 - A name, likely referring to a man or a family.
  • 王孙 - A title, referring to a noble youth or prince.
  • 五可 - A name, likely referring to a woman.
  • 桂庵 - A name, likely referring to a man or a family.
  • 张 - A name, likely referring to a man or a family.

[1663]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 王孫周旋兩間 - Wang Sun (王孫) refers to a noble descendant, and '周旋兩間' means 'to navigate between two spaces or situations,' implying a state of indecision or uncertainty.
  • 蹀踱無以自處 - '蹀踱' (dié duó) means 'to pace back and forth,' and '無以自處' means 'unable to find a place for oneself,' indicating a sense of being lost or unsure of one's position.
  • 母乃調停於中 - '母' (mǔ) means 'mother,' and '調停於中' means 'to mediate or intervene in the middle,' suggesting the mother's role in resolving the situation.
  • 使序行以齒 - '使' (shǐ) means 'to make or cause,' '序行' (xù xíng) means 'to arrange in order,' and '以齒' (yǐ chǐ) means 'by age,' indicating that the mother arranges them by age.
  • 二女皆諾 - '二女' (èr nǚ) means 'two girls,' and '皆諾' (jiē nuò) means 'both agreed,' indicating their consent.
  • 五可聞閨秀差長 - '五可' (wǔ kě) is a name, '聞' (wén) means 'to hear or know,' '閨秀' (guī xiù) means 'lady or woman of refinement,' and '差長' (chā zhǎng) means 'slightly older,' indicating that Wukě learns that the other girl is slightly older.
  • 稱“姊”有難色 - '稱' (chēng) means 'to call or address,' '姊' (zǐ) means 'older sister,' and '有難色' (yǒu nán sè) means 'showed reluctance or hesitation,' indicating that Wukě hesitates to call her 'sister.'
  • 母甚慮之 - '母' (mǔ) means 'mother,' '甚' (shèn) means 'very,' and '慮之' (lǜ zhī) means 'worried about it,' indicating the mother's concern.
  • 比三朝公會 - '比' (bǐ) means 'by the time,' '三朝' (sān zhāo) means 'three days after,' and '公會' (gōng huì) means 'public gathering,' indicating a specific event.
  • 五可見閨秀風致宜人 - '五可' (wǔ kě) is a name, '見' (jiàn) means 'to see,' '閨秀' (guī xiù) means 'lady or woman of refinement,' '風致' (fēng zhì) means 'graceful appearance,' and '宜人' (yí rén) means 'pleasing to look at,' indicating that Wukě finds the other girl's appearance pleasant.
  • 不覺右之 - '不覺' (bù jué) means 'unconsciously,' and '右之' (yòu zhī) means 'to favor or prefer,' indicating that Wukě unconsciously favors the other girl.
  • 自是始定 - '自是' (zì shì) means 'from then on,' and '始定' (shǐ dìng) means 'began to be settled,' indicating that the relationship became more stable.
  • 然父母恐其積久不相能 - '然' (rán) means 'however,' '父母' (fù mǔ) means 'parents,' '恐' (kǒng) means 'fear,' '其' (qí) means 'that,' '積久' (jī jiǔ) means 'over time,' and '不相能' (bù xiāng néng) means 'unable to get along,' indicating the parents' fear that they might not get along over time.
  • 而二女卻無間言 - '而' (ér) means 'but,' '二女' (èr nǚ) means 'two girls,' '卻' (què) means 'however,' and '無間言' (wú jiàn yán) means 'no words of discord,' indicating that the two girls had no conflicts.
  • 衣履易着 - '衣履' (yī lǚ) means 'clothes and shoes,' and '易着' (yì zhe) means 'easy to wear,' indicating that they dressed similarly.
  • 相爱如姊妹焉 - '相爱' (xiāng ài) means 'to love each other,' '如姊妹' (rú zǐ mèi) means 'like sisters,' and '焉' (yān) is a particle indicating a completed action, indicating that they loved each other like sisters.
  • 王孫始問五可却媒之故 - '王孫' (wáng sūn) means 'Wang Sun,' '始問' (shǐ wèn) means 'began to ask,' '五可' (wǔ kě) is a name, '却媒' (què méi) means 'rejected the matchmaker,' and '之故' (zhī gù) means 'the reason,' indicating that Wang Sun began to ask Wukě why she rejected the matchmaker.
  • 笑曰 - '笑曰' (xiào yuē) means 'said with a smile,' indicating a lighthearted tone.
  • 無他，聊報君之却于媪耳 - '無他' (wú tā) means 'nothing else,' '聊' (liáo) means 'just,' '報' (bào) means 'to repay,' '君' (jūn) means 'you,' '却于媪' (què yú ǎo) means 'rejected the matchmaker,' and '耳' (ěr) means 'just,' indicating that Wukě's response was just to repay Wang Sun for rejecting the matchmaker.
  • 尚未见妾 - '尚未' (shàng wèi) means 'not yet,' '见' (jiàn) means 'to see,' and '妾' (qiè) means 'I' (used by a woman), indicating that Wang Sun had not yet seen Wukě.
  • 意中止有闺秀 - '意中' (yì zhōng) means 'in one's mind,' '止' (zhǐ) means 'only,' and '有闺秀' (yǒu guī xiù) means 'there is a lady of refinement,' indicating that Wang Sun only thought of the other girl.
  • 即见妾，亦略靳之 - '即' (jí) means 'if,' '见' (jiàn) means 'to see,' '妾' (qiè) means 'I' (used by a woman), '亦' (yì) means 'also,' '略' (lüè) means 'slightly,' and '靳之' (jìn zhī) means 'to be stingy or reserved with,' indicating that even if he saw Wukě, he would be slightly reserved.
  • 以觇君之视妾，较闺秀何如也 - '以觇' (yǐ chān) means 'to observe,' '君' (jūn) means 'you,' '视妾' (shì qiè) means 'seeing me,' '较' (jiào) means 'compared to,' '闺秀' (guī xiù) means 'lady of refinement,' and '何如也' (hé rú yě) means 'how is it,' indicating that Wukě wanted to see how Wang Sun would compare her to the other girl.
  • 使君为伊病，而不为妾病 - '使' (shǐ) means 'if,' '君' (jūn) means 'you,' '为伊病' (wèi yī bìng) means 'sick for her,' '而' (ér) means 'but,' '不为妾病' (bù wèi qiè bìng) means 'not sick for me,' indicating that if Wang Sun was sick for the other girl but not for Wukě.
  • 则亦不必强求容矣 - '则' (zé) means 'then,' '亦' (yì) means 'also,' '不必' (bù bì) means 'not necessary,' '强求' (qiǎng qiú) means 'to force,' and '容' (róng) means 'acceptance,' indicating that it would not be necessary to force acceptance.
  • 王孙笑曰 - '王孙' (wáng sūn) means 'Wang Sun,' '笑曰' (xiào yuē) means 'said with a smile,' indicating a lighthearted tone.
